返回日报
Hacker News #8

GLM 5.2 和即将到来的人工智能利润率崩溃

GLM 5.2 and the coming AI margin collapse

AI 作者 martinald 热度 565 回复 342 2026/07/07 04:14
打开原帖 打开外部链接 JSON

摘要

  • This is a two part series focusing on what I believe is perhaps the least understood upcoming shift in AI economics. If you've enjoyed this and want to be notified about the second post, please feel free to sign up for my newsletter. The real DeepSeek moment is upon us What feels like decades ago, markets recoiled at DeepSeek's R1 model. The theory being that given the underlying V3 model reportedly cost under $6m to train, the market therefore thought the huge investment in capex for model training was over, and thus the stock price of Nvidia et al collapsed overnight. Of course, this was a hugely poor read of where the costs actually lie in AI. Training - while no doubt capex intensive - is a fixed, up-front cost. You spend hundreds of millions to train a model, then you are "done".[1] Inference, on the other hand, scales with your demand. It has genuine marginal costs. I've written about this at length over the past year or so. Again, the mainstream understanding of this - that the API costs the providers charge are their real costs is mistaken. Indeed, when Anthropic/OpenAI charge $25/MTok for inference, my napkin maths suggests that this is probably something like 90% gross margin on the cost of compute vs the rack rate. It may be a bit higher, or a bit lower (OpenAI's leaked financials suggest a ~60% gross margin on revenue, but this no doubt includes a lot of other costs like support, payment processing and other services they offer), but the whole business model of frontier AI labs is in short to spend a large amount of money on salaries on compute to train a model, then amortise that cost over a lot of very profitable inference. If you can amortise that cost over enough inference you turn from profitable on a COGS basis to... actually profitable.
  • I have been playing around with GLM5.2 from Z.ai for the last couple of weeks. I believe GLM5.2 is the first model that reaches the "bar" of a genuine open weights competitor to Opus and GPT (at the time of writing, the latest version of GPT was 5.5 - future models no doubt will exceed this). It's genuinely very good and hard for me to tell the difference between Opus - my daily driver and it. I've found that it is slow because of the amount of thinking it tends to do. For non interactive agentic tasks (like reviewing PRs in the background) which aren't time critical this is a non issue, but for interactive use it is definitely a tad too slow to keep my attention. This also somewhat reduces the cost effectiveness of it (more thinking means more tokens, which increases costs). It also doesn't have vision support. It's funny how quickly I've gone from basically never wanting to use vision (because it was so inaccurate, I'd often pause sessions when I caught it using vision), to using it all the time - since Opus 4.7 introduced far higher resolution vision capabilities. It's genuinely frustrating it not being able to read image-based PDFs, screenshots and design files. I'm sure they have a more multimodal model in the works, but this is a significant weakness against the frontier labs. Secondly, and something I really didn't expect to be a blocker, is the lack of/poor web search capabilities. It turns out that nearly every agentic session does a lot of web searching for looking up items. Z.ai provides a replacement MCP for web search, but it's pretty awful and slow. Fireworks doesn't provide any, though they gave me a very vague answer saying they are always looking to improve products. I would take that as no plans personally, but let's see. I've managed to somewhat work around this by telling the agent to use a CLI based web search like ddgr, but this is a real weakness right now. I am very bullish on the potential of 3rd party web search APIs. This is actually a huge gap in what open weights model providers can offer, and it turns out great web search capabilities are essential for many agentic tasks. Regardless, this no doubt will be solved with time - there are many people building web search indexes and it just requires the right partnerships and plumbing in place. Drop in replacement Where it gets really scary for the frontier labs is how easy it is to migrate to open weights models. Both Z.ai and Fireworks offer both an OpenAI compatible and Anthropic compatible endpoint. This makes it absolutely trivial to use with Claude Code and Codex. You just set the base URL to point to your inference provider, give it the API key and tell it to use GLM5.2. Given Anthropic recently announced (then backtracked) on charging API rates for claude -p non interactive agentic use, you will find for many/most of those use cases you can just drop in GLM instead. And for interactive use, apart from the lack of vision and slow(er) speed[2], it was genuinely almost impossible for me to realise I wasn't using Opus in Claude Code. This is not Microsoft or Salesforce like lock in, where you need to spend years planning a migration. The switching costs are incredibly low, and I would argue that are actually far less than trying to keep up on all the policy and term changes that the frontier lab models tend to scramble around with. It's possible that Claude Code will make it harder to use 3rd party providers, but there are many good open source options (like Codex itself and OpenCode, amongst dozens). One concern I do hear from enterprise is data privacy and security. There is no doubt that using Z.ai's official API and subscription is almost certainly a non-starter, with their terms being at best weak and the deep connection to Mainland China. But of course, with open weights being open there are many other providers in the market, many with proper contractual provisions. And, if that isn't enough, you can of course host in on premises yourself, which actually opens up even more sensitive data - that couldn't be sent to any third party - to Opus-quality agentic workflows. Cost savings The going rate for GLM5.2 seems to be around the $4.40/MTok mark. This is less than 20% of the retail price of Opus and ~15% the cost of GPT5.5. Now, given it does use more tokens for a given task, this isn't a totally apples to apples comparison. But I'd be very surprised if it wasn't more than 50% cheaper for nearly all workflows, for a very similar level of quality. In terms of subscriptions, Z.ai offers a "coding plan" subscription which mirrors the plans you'd see from Anthropic and OpenAI, but with a higher claimed usage limit. I expect for most professional use the very lax terms around training and data retention will make this a difficult sell, but if the frontier labs were to try and increase pricing substantially I can see it being a credible option for those that are budget-conscious. I expect these costs for GLM5.2 to come down significantly over the coming months as well, as more optimisation is done to the serving stack(s). Wafer wrote an interesting write up of their efforts to run it on AMD hardware. They suggest that it is 2.75x cheaper per token to run inference on AMD vs Nvidia Blackwell. Part two is where this gets interesting - what a collapse in inference margins actually does to the industry, and who is likely to win and lose. I'd keep Bezos's famous "your margin is my opportunity" line in mind. If you'd like me to drop it in your inbox the moment it's out, sign up to the newsletter - or grab the RSS feed if that's more your thing. Disclosure - Fireworks kindly gave me some free credit to experiment with GLM to help write this article.

命中 6 个 AI/科技关键词。

davedx 2026/07/07 21:28
同时：

>过去一个月，中国商务部牵头与阿里巴巴、字节跳动和http://z.ai/等主要人工智能公司举行会议，讨论限制海外获取尖端人工智能模型（包括尚未发布的模型）的措施。

>讨论的内容不仅包括据称的闭源模型，还包括开放权重模型。

>未来的监管可能采取基于技术能力的分层框架的形式。基本的开源人工智能模型可能会通过备案系统进行管理，高性能模型可能会受到安全审查，最敏感的前沿模型可能会被禁止公开发布或限制在中国境内使用

https://www.reuters.com/world/beijing-is-looking-curbing-ove...

Meanwhile:

> China’s Ministry of Commerce has led meetings over the past month with major AI companies, including Alibaba, ByteDance, and http://z.ai/, to discuss measures that would restrict overseas access to cutting-edge AI models, including models that have not yet been released.

> The discussions reportedly include not only closed-source models but also open-weight models.

> Future regulations could take the form of a tiered framework based on technological capability. Basic open-source AI models may be managed through a filing system, high-performance models may be subject to security reviews, and the most sensitive frontier models may be banned from public release or restricted to use within China

https://www.reuters.com/world/beijing-is-looking-curbing-ove...

很难区分哪些是宣传，哪些不是。

例如，您在此处引用的这家路透社都讲述了制造恐惧、不确定性和助长宣传的迹象。但话又说回来，我也不能确定。

Reporting/news around AI is so very interesting these days.

It's really hard to tell what's propaganda from what's not.

It's completely the opposite stance to (a) the actions being taken by chinese companies and (b) the public stance taken by their govt https://english.www.gov.cn/news/202601/08/content_WS695f1b55...

Lalabadie 2026/07/07 22:52
他们不会吗？他们从人工智能提供商的每次订阅中获利，因为所有公司都是国有的。

他们还处于有利地位，可以通过提供与市场领导者声称的只有万亿级支出才能实现的开放模型相匹配的开放模型来控制突破性的美国人工智能公司如何向投资者展示。考虑到支撑美国股市的股票是多么少，以及这些股票都依赖于相同的信念和因素，这是对美国经济的强有力的控制。

此外，就中国的总体科技投资而言，五年计划只是......可以阅读。这不是秘密。它解释了该战略，您可以从该计划与他们现在丰富的太阳能基础设施和技术成就（包括专门命名的人工智能）直接联系起来。

https://cset.georgetown.edu/publication/china-14th-five-year...

They won't? They're profiting from every subscription to AI providers, because all companies are state-owned.

They're also well-positioned to control how groundbreaking US AI companies are allowed to appear to their investors, by offering open models that match what market leaders claim can only be attained with trillion-level spend. That's a strong control on US economy, considering how few stocks are propping up the US stock market, and how these stocks are all dependent on the same beliefs and factors.

Also, regarding China's tech investment in general, the five-year plan is just... available to read. It's not a secret. It explains the strategy, and you can draw a direct line from the plan to their now abundant solar infrastructures and tech achievements, including in AI, which is specifically named.

https://cset.georgetown.edu/publication/china-14th-five-year...

楼层
WarmWash 2026/07/07 23:07
中国的五年计划是习近平想要的任何东西。这只是一个正式的指导方针，而不是人们可以起诉中共的法律或宪法。

此外，即使中国决定不想将生产力皇冠上的宝石留在国内，美国也将禁止其进口。也许 XzeRo_337 将进行种子下载并拥有 VPN 来访问外国提供商，但 Timmy 和 Ashley 只会支付他们的 ChatGPT 订阅费用，Mega corp 将支付他们的 Claude Legend 代币费用。

China's five year plan is whatever Xi wants whenever he wants it. It's just a formal guideline, not a law or constitution people can sue the CCP over.

Also, even if China decides it doesn't want to keep the crown jewels of productivity close to home, the US will ban their import. Maybe XzeRo_337 will be torrenting weights and have a VPN to access foreign providers, but Timmy and Ashley are just going to pay for their ChatGPT subs, and Mega corp will pay their Claude Legend token expenses.

Lalabadie 2026/07/07 23:51
你正在将美国文化逻辑应用于中国官僚体系
You're applying US cultural logic to Chinese bureaucracy
楼层
WarmWash 2026/07/08 00:04
中国是一个独裁国家，只有一位领导人，对生活拥有单方面无可争议的控制权。

中国没有真正的官僚机构（或任何其他与此相关的结构），因为归根结底，中国是一个可以随时随地为所欲为的人。对于平民和官员来说，这种假官僚机构是存在的，但对于顶层决策的精英来说，这个假官僚机构是零。

如果习近平不希望模型出口，他就不会派一个合法代表团去中国最高法院为他的裁决而战。无论其他人或该国的任何文件说什么，它都会发生，并且任何不喜欢它的人都可以寻求零追索权。

China is an authoritarian state with a single leader who has unilateral uncontested control for life.

China has no real bureaucracy (or any other structure for that matter) because at the end of the day, it's one guy who can do whatever he wants whenever he wants. For commoners and officials there is this faux bureaucracy, but for the elite at the top making decisions, there is zero.

If Xi doesn't want models exported, he's not having a legal delegation go to the supreme court of China to fight for his ruling. It just happens, regardless of whatever anyone else or any piece of paper in the country says, and there is zero recourse anyone who doesn't like it can pursue.

十大太监，如果你想举个例子的话。也就是说，他们的官僚机构始终与单一领导人合作，这些领导人表面上对生活拥有单方面无争议的控制权。 https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ten_Attendants

More or less authoritarian than Imperial dynasties? Zhao Gao for Qin, Huang Hao for Shu Han, Yang Fugong for Tang, this list can just keep going.

The Ten Eunuchs, if you want one example. Which is to say, their bureaucracies have always worked with single leaders who ostensibly had unilateral uncontested control for life. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ten_Attendants

myrmidon 2026/07/07 21:56
这纯粹是猜测，说实话。

您也可以将欧洲的做法视为前沿模型将无法在公开竞争中保持显着优势的赌注（因此不值得对其进行补贴，因为任何经济优势充其量都是转瞬即逝的）。

从数据和相关的过去经验来看，这看起来是一个相当可靠的赌注（尽管“风险”很难预测）量化）。

This is pure speculation at this point tbh.

You could just as well read the european approach as a bet that frontier models will be unable to keep a significant edge over open competition (and thus not worth throwing subsidies at, because any economic advantage is fleeting at best).

Looking at the data and related past experience, this looks like a pretty solid bet (despite the "risk" being hard to quantify).

楼层
sajithdilshan 2026/07/07 22:08
> 您也可以将欧洲的做法视为前沿模型将无法在公开竞争中保持显着优势的赌注

这是他们将输掉 100% 的赌注。一旦中国开始实施出口禁令/控制对其模型的访问，欧洲将受到美国/中国的摆布，允许他们访问或仅仅依赖悲惨的米斯特拉尔

> You could just as well read the european approach as a bet that frontier models will be unable to keep a significant edge over open competition

And that's a bet they will lose 100%. Once the Chinese starts imposing export bans/controlling the access to their models, Europe would be at the mercy of US/China to allow them access or just rely on miserable mistral

楼层
myrmidon 2026/07/07 22:20
您再次假设前沿模型将长期保持有意义的领先地位。如果情况并非如此，那么出口管制/禁令就毫无意义。

有大量强有力的指标表明它们不会保持领先地位：假设任何形式的技术进步都遵循某种形式的物流功能（其中“收益”，在本例中为“智能”在某个时刻变成亚线性）是（长期）一个非常保守且经过验证的假设，并且“自动”否定您的领先优势

同样，在密码学/计算机国际象棋/算法设计等学科中，纯粹的“智力”优势也从未真正保持集中。

Again, you are assuming that frontier models will stay meaningfully ahead long-term. Export controls/bans are pointless if this is not the case.

There is ton of strong indicators that they will not stay ahead: Assuming that technological progress of any kind follows some form of logistic function (where "gains", in this case "intelligence" become sub-linear at some point) is (long-term) a very conservative and proven assumption, and "automatically" negates your lead over time.

Similarly, purely "intellectual" advantage in disciplines like cryptography/computer chess/algorithm design never really stayed concentrated, either.

楼层
peterbell_nyc 2026/07/07 22:51
我会选择这个赌注的另一面。虽然我同意任何给定进步的影响都可能类似于 S 型曲线，但我认为“堆叠 S 型曲线”很有可能创造出看起来指数级的东西。

举一个简单的例子，看看过去约 500 年的技术进步 - 在我看来，尽管许多逻辑曲线在此过程中变平，但变化的速度仍在继续加快。

关于堆叠是否会带来巨大的未解答的问题乙状结肠将有利于现任者。但我不会明确地与拥有最多计算数据、人才和资金的人打赌。

I would take the other side of this bet. While I agree that the impact of any given advance is likely to resemble a sigmoid curve, I think there is a material chance of "stacking sigmoids" creating something that looks exponential.

To take a simple example, look at the progress of technology over the last ~500 years - it seems to me that the rate of change continues to accelerate despite many of the logistic curves flattening along the way.

There are still huge unanswered questions about whether or not the stacking sigmoids will favor the incumbents. But I would not definitively bet against the people with the most compute data, talent and money.

楼层
myrmidon 2026/07/07 23:01
我不太确定您所说的“S型曲线叠加”具体指什么。

我认为许多重要技术（比如电路设计！）在上个世纪基本上是从零开始的，并且在相当长的一段时间内，其发展实际上是呈指数级的（=> 因为我们在曲线上的起点非常低）。

但是，如果将事物简化为单一指标（人均财富？人类可用的总能源？全球工业/建筑产出以吨计？），我想不出有什么例子表明这种“叠加”成功颠覆了S型曲线趋势（或者从长期来看有这种迹象）。

I'm unsure what exactly you mean by sigmoid stacking.

I'd argue that a lot of important technologies (like circuit design!) started at basically zero in the last century, and the progress was actually exponential for a significant time (=> because we started so low on the curve).

But if you reduce things to a single metric (wealth per person? total energy available to humanity? global industrial/construction output in tons?) I can't think of anything where such "stacking" successful subverted the sigmoid trend (or looks like it will, long-term).

楼层
sajithdilshan 2026/07/07 22:30
这不是智力优势，而是能力优势。假设欧洲将获得他们所需的关于如何训练前沿模型的尖端技术的所有知识（他们不会，因为美国和中国会将此作为国家机密），谁将建立基础设施来训练模型？谁将建设数据中心（多年项目）以及谁将建设能源基础设施？仅仅因为您知道如何做到这一点并不意味着您也有能力做到这一点。

最好的例子之一就是核反应堆。到目前为止，相关知识和技术已经相当成熟和开放，但并不是每个国家都能建造核反应堆。前沿模型也是如此。

欧盟应该已经开始投资基础设施方面，以防他们获得专业知识，但你们的政客们仍在养老金改革和乌克兰战争等问题上争论不休。

It is not about intellectual advantage, but about capabilities as well. Assuming that Europe would get all the knowledge they need on the cutting edge technology on how to train a frontier model (which they won't because US and China would guard this as national secrets), who is going to setup infrastructure to train the model? Who is building the data centers (multi-year project) and who is going to build the energy infrastructure? Just because you know how to do it doesn't mean you have the capability to do that as well.

One of the best example is nuclear reactors. By now the know how and technology is fairly mature and open, but not every country gets to build nuclear reactors. Same would be with the frontier models as well.

EU should have already started investing in the infrastructure side in-case they obtain the know how, but your politicians are still bickering on pension reforms and Ukrainian war, etc.

楼层
nightski 2026/07/07 22:47
那么Deepmind/Meta等公司……难道都必须切断他们在欧盟的办事处，尽管那里拥有令人难以置信的人才？我只是觉得这种情况不太可能发生。
So Deepmind/Meta/etc... would all have to cut off their EU offices even though they hold incredible talent? I'm just not finding this scenario likely.
楼层
sajithdilshan 2026/07/07 22:56
Meta在欧盟有AI训练数据中心吗？

他们完全可以继续运营并提供正常的服务访问权限，只需屏蔽AI访问即可。这种情况已经在发生了，苹果就不会在欧盟发布新版Siri（诚然，这是由于法规冲突），但这将是一个试探点。

如果欧洲人仍然要为劣于美国同类产品的服务/产品支付相同的价格，那对这些公司来说就是双赢的局面。只需在欧盟以虚高的价格出售功能缩减的非AI版本服务/产品即可。

Does meta has an AI training data center in EU?

They can just operate and provide normal access to their services, just block AI access. This is already happening, apple would not release new Siri in EU (granted it's due to a regulation clash) but this would be a testing point.

If Europeans are still paying the same prices for sub par services/products to their American counter parts, it's win-win situation for those companies. Just sell a dumbed down non-AI version of service/product in EU for an inflated price.

楼层
nightski 2026/07/07 23:22
Meta的FAIR在欧盟确实设有多个研发办事处。所以您的意思是，他们的实验室可以在欧盟进行模型的研发，甚至可能在那里训练模型，只是不能进行生产环境的大语言模型推理服务或发布模型权重？我只是觉得这说不通。

AI/ML研究界有相当大一部分人都在欧盟。

Meta's FAIR has several R&D offices in the EU, yes. So you are saying their labs can conduct R&D on models in the EU, potentially even train them there, they just can't have production LLM inference serving or release the model weights? I'm just not seeing it.

A not-insignificant portion of the AI/ML research community is in the EU.

楼层
